title: Mario Amendola

infobox.value: Mario Natalino Concetto Amendola  (1910-12-08)8 December 1910  [Recco](/wiki/Recco "Recco"), [Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy")

[Ferruccio Amendola](/wiki/Ferruccio_Amendola "Ferruccio Amendola") (nephew) [Claudio Amendola](/wiki/Claudio_Amendola "Claudio Amendola") (great-nephew) [Alessia Amendola](/wiki/Alessia_Amendola "Alessia Amendola") (great-great-niece)
(8 December 1910 – 22 December 1993) was an Italian screenwriter, film director and dramatist.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian screenwriter (1910 –1993)

| Mario Amendola | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | Mario Natalino Concetto Amendola  (1910-12-08)8 December 1910  [Recco](/wiki/Recco "Recco"), [Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") |
| Died | 22 December 1993(1993-12-22) (aged 83)  [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Italy |
| Occupations | * Screenwriter * film director |
| Years active | 1931–1987 |
| Relatives | [Ferruccio Amendola](/wiki/Ferruccio_Amendola "Ferruccio Amendola") (nephew) [Claudio Amendola](/wiki/Claudio_Amendola "Claudio Amendola") (great-nephew) [Alessia Amendola](/wiki/Alessia_Amendola "Alessia Amendola") (great-great-niece) |

**Mario Natalino Concetto Amendola** (8 December 1910 – 22 December 1993) was an Italian screenwriter, film director and dramatist.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mario_Amendola&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Amendola was born in [Recco](/wiki/Recco "Recco"), which is located in [Genova](/wiki/Genova "Genova") to parents from Rome. He began his career on stage in 1931 as a writer for [avanspettacolo shows](/wiki/Avanspettacolo "Avanspettacolo"). During that time, he wrote dialogue for [Erminio Macario](/wiki/Erminio_Macario "Erminio Macario"), [Totò](/wiki/Tot%C3%B2 "Totò") and more. He ended this profession and moved to cinema in 1949. He wrote for 153 films between 1941 and 1987. He also directed 37 films between 1949 and 1975.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Amendola collaborated with [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") on conceiving the Nico Giraldi film series starring [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Amendola died on December 12, 1993,[[3]](#cite_note-3) from complications from [diabetes](/wiki/Diabetes "Diabetes").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
